thesis: Ouster is a digital-lidar pure play positioning itself as a leader in sensing and perception for 'Physical AI' — the 3D eyes for robots, autonomous machines, ADAS, and smart infrastructure. Revenue is growing fast (FY2025 +52% to $169M; Q1 2026 +49%) as its OS sensor family and new Rev8 native-color lidar ramp, aided by the Stereolabs camera acquisition, BlueCity smart-traffic systems, and design wins across heavy-equipment autonomy (AIM) and transportation (NJ DOT). Analysts are bullish (Strong Buy, ~$49 target, ~32% upside), and multiple 2026 equity raises leave the balance sheet well funded on a stated path to profitability by 2027. But this is a speculative name: Ouster is still loss-making, the stock carries an extreme ~3.2 beta and a $16-64 52-week range, lidar is a brutally competitive and historically value-destructive industry, and recent raises are dilutive. Positive thesis, high uncertainty — a SPEC. BUY for risk-tolerant investors.

drivers: Rev8 native-color digital lidar and the BlueCity smart-infrastructure platform drive revenue past $250M+ with expanding gross margins
Physical AI adoption accelerates across robotics, heavy-equipment autonomy (AIM Intelligent Machines), logistics, and intelligent transportation
Ouster reaches profitability at or ahead of its 2027 target, validating the operating-leverage story
Domestic-procurement tailwinds (Build America, Buy America compliance) win smart-city and government infrastructure deals
The market re-rates Ouster as the scaled, IP-rich digital-lidar leader in a consolidating industry

base_case.assumptions: Ouster keeps growing revenue ~40-50% as OS and Rev8 sensors ramp across industrial, robotics, and smart-infrastructure markets, with gross margin expanding toward the mid-40s and the path to 2027 profitability intact. The well-funded balance sheet covers the remaining cash burn. The stock reaches the ~$49 analyst consensus (Oppenheimer at $57) over the next year as growth and margin progress are delivered.

risks: Lidar remains brutally competitive (Hesai, RoboSense, Luminar, Innoviz, plus camera/radar alternatives), pressuring price and share
Continued losses force further dilutive capital raises, compounding the recent share issuance
Automotive/ADAS and AV demand disappoints or design wins fail to convert to volume revenue
Growth decelerates and the ~13x sales multiple compresses sharply given no profits
Extreme volatility (~3.2 beta) drives a large drawdown in any risk-off rotation out of speculative Physical AI names

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — product revenue growth, gross margin, cash burn, and profitability-path commentary
Rev8 native-color lidar ramp and high-volume production with Benchmark Electronics
BlueCity intelligent-transportation deployments (e.g., NJ DOT / FIFA World Cup 2026) and Build America, Buy America-driven infrastructure wins
Physical-AI design wins in robotics and heavy-equipment autonomy (AIM Intelligent Machines) and the Stereolabs camera integration
Stated path to profitability — evidence of operating leverage and cash-flow breakeven

key_risks: Unprofitable: still loss-making with a stated profitability target only in 2027
Dilution: multiple 2026 equity offerings have added shares, and further raises are possible if losses persist
Intense competition in a historically value-destructive lidar industry, plus camera/radar substitution
Extreme volatility: ~3.2 beta and a $16-64 52-week range make this a high-risk holding
Valuation: ~$2.5B market cap on ~$185M TTM revenue (~13x sales) with no profits leaves little margin for error
Dependence on early-stage end markets (AV, robotics, smart infrastructure) whose adoption timing is uncertain
